upcoming.expectation: The June report is historically a quiet one for corn and soybeans. May already delivered the first new-crop balance sheets, and the year's real acreage shake-up doesn't land until the June 30 Acreage and Grain Stocks reports - so the trade is looking for only minor old-crop demand tweaks here. May left old-crop carryout at 2.14 bil bu corn and 340 mil bu soybeans; new-crop 2026/27 sits at 1.96 bil bu corn and 310 mil bu soybeans after a tight first look. With no acreage update due, new-crop changes this month hinge on early-season weather and the export pace.

history.reaction: Smallest winter wheat crop in six decades on Southern Plains drought - July KC wheat ran limit-up and futures hit 2-yr highs.
Trade looked for a higher carryout; the tighter print pushed Nov beans to a 2-yr high near $12.
New-crop carryout landed near the trade guess - ample supply into 2027 capped the upside.
Old-crop carryout close to the trade estimate - little reaction.
Old-crop carryout in line - the week's move came from the new-crop sheets.
